title: Strict Theorem-and-Proof Rigor Audit Rules
description: Strict rules for mathematical correctness auditing: hypotheses, quantifiers, scope, proof structure, justification, case completeness, and conclusion matching.

provenance.basis: Curated engineering rules for formal math auditing; similar in spirit to proof-checking checklists, but aimed at mathematical validity.

rules.title: State all hypotheses explicitly
rules.check: Flag theorem statements whose conclusions rely on unstated assumptions: topology, boundary conditions, initialization, or parameter ranges.
rules.pass_criteria: All assumptions needed by the proof are stated in the theorem/lemma statement, the local setup, or an explicitly inherited standing assumptions block.
failure_modes: Regularity used later but absent from the statement
rules.fix_template: Add the missing assumptions to the theorem statement or explicitly cite standing assumptions.

rules.title: Quantifiers and domains must be explicit
rules.check: Flag free variables, ambiguous quantifier order, or missing domains in theorem statements and proof steps.
rules.pass_criteria: Every variable is bound or declared, and quantifier order matches the intended claim.
rules.fix_template: Rewrite the statement to make all quantifiers and domains explicit (e.g., “for all x∈X” / “there exists δ>0”).

rules.title: No undefined objects inside proofs
rules.check: Flag objects introduced without definition: operators, norms, measures, constants, or sets.
rules.pass_criteria: Each object introduced is defined with enough detail to make subsequent steps checkable.
failure_modes: Using an operator T without specifying its domain/codomain
Introducing a constant C without stating dependence
rules.fix_template: Define the object when introduced (domain/codomain/assumptions), or cite where it was defined.

rules.title: Proof must close exactly the stated claim
rules.check: Compare the final line of the proof to the theorem claim and flag partial, stronger, weaker, or shifted conclusions.
rules.pass_criteria: The proof closes the exact theorem claim under the stated assumptions.
failure_modes: Special case proved instead of general claim
rules.fix_template: Either strengthen the proof or weaken the theorem statement to what is actually proved.

rules.title: Every nontrivial inference step must be justified
rules.check: Flag leaps using therefore, hence, thus, implies, or 'it follows' where justification is absent and not standard from the immediately preceding line.
rules.pass_criteria: Each nontrivial step cites a theorem, lemma, definition, inequality, algebraic manipulation, or clearly checkable argument.
failure_modes: Exchange of limit and integral with no theorem
rules.fix_template: Add the missing justification or split the leap into smaller verified steps.

rules.title: Avoid variable capture and scope errors
rules.check: Flag reuse of the same symbol for different instantiated objects within overlapping scope; flag variables that escape their quantifier scope.
rules.pass_criteria: Bound variables remain local; newly instantiated objects get fresh names; reused dummy indices are confined to local formulas.
failure_modes: Using k for two different existentials
Treating a bound variable as globally fixed
rules.fix_template: Rename variables to avoid clashes and restate the instantiation step explicitly.

rules.title: Existence proofs must not assume the object being proved
rules.check: Flag “let x be such that …” when the existence of x is the conclusion, not a hypothesis.
rules.pass_criteria: Existence is established by construction or by citing an existence theorem; then the object is named.
rules.fix_template: Either construct the object or cite the theorem that guarantees existence before introducing the symbol.

rules.title: Case splits must be exhaustive and clearly structured
rules.check: Flag proofs that say “consider cases” without stating all cases or without showing they cover all possibilities.
rules.pass_criteria: Cases cover the entire domain and are either disjoint or overlap handled explicitly.
failure_modes: Case structure not connected to the goal
rules.fix_template: List the cases explicitly and add a one-line argument that they cover all possibilities.

rules.title: Induction proofs must include base case and induction step
rules.check: Flag induction proofs missing base case(s), missing induction hypothesis, or unclear induction variable domain.
rules.pass_criteria: Base case(s) verified; induction hypothesis stated; induction step proved; conclusion applies to intended domain.
failure_modes: Strong induction used without stating range
rules.fix_template: Add explicit base case, IH, and step; clarify whether induction is weak/strong and the starting index.

rules.title: Proof by contradiction must exhibit a clear contradiction
rules.check: Flag contradiction proofs that never state an explicit false statement or that end with 'contradiction' without identifying it.
rules.pass_criteria: The negation is assumed; a concrete contradiction (C and not C, or an explicitly false inequality) is derived.
failure_modes: Contradiction is asserted but not shown
rules.fix_template: State the assumed negation explicitly and identify the precise contradictory statements at the end.

rules.title: Iff / equivalence proofs must prove both directions
rules.check: Flag claims of equivalence with only one implication proved or the reverse direction missing.
rules.pass_criteria: Both P⇒Q and Q⇒P are proved (or an equivalent characterization is established).
rules.fix_template: Add the missing direction in a separate paragraph or restructure as two implications.

rules.title: Avoid 'obvious/clearly/trivial' without explanation
rules.check: Flag words: obvious, clearly, trivial, immediate, evident when used to replace reasoning.
rules.pass_criteria: If such words are used, a short reason follows, or the word is removed.
exceptions: Very standard algebraic step with a cited lemma or definition
Venue where such phrasing is explicitly acceptable (still discouraged)
rules.fix_template: Delete the word or add a short justification sentence (1–2 lines).

rules.title: Proof should indicate where hypotheses are used
rules.check: Flag long proofs where assumptions appear in statement but are never referenced or used in reasoning.
rules.pass_criteria: Major assumptions are invoked at least once in the proof, or explicitly stated as unused/redundant.
failure_modes: Hypothesis used implicitly but never mentioned
rules.fix_template: Insert a sentence indicating where each key hypothesis is applied, or remove redundant hypotheses.

rules.title: No silent strengthening/weakening of quantifiers in proof
rules.check: Flag steps that change “for all” to “for some”, or replace arbitrary element with a special one without justification.
rules.pass_criteria: Quantifier changes are explicitly justified (e.g., choose a generic element, then fix it).
failure_modes: Choosing a convenient x without loss justification
Proving existence for a specific parameter rather than arbitrary
rules.fix_template: Restate the quantifier structure and ensure the proof introduces elements in the correct order (generic then specific).

rules.title: Equality/inequality chains must be logically annotated when nontrivial
rules.check: Flag long chains of equalities/inequalities without stating which theorem or property justifies each step, especially when steps are not algebraic identities.
rules.pass_criteria: Nontrivial steps cite the reason (monotonicity, Jensen, triangle inequality, dominated convergence, etc.).
failure_modes: Measure-theoretic limit step with no condition
rules.fix_template: Break the chain and annotate each nontrivial transformation with the justification.

rules.title: Definitions must precede the theorems that use them
rules.check: Flag theorems using a defined term that has not been defined yet in the document order.
rules.pass_criteria: All nonstandard terms are defined before first usage in theorem statements; if deferred, a Notation/Preliminaries section is referenced explicitly.
rules.fix_template: Move the definition earlier or add a forward reference to the Notation section and ensure it exists.

rules.title: Uniqueness language requires proof of uniqueness
rules.check: Flag usage of “the solution”, “the minimizer”, “the fixed point” when uniqueness has not been proved.
rules.pass_criteria: Use “a” unless uniqueness is established or cited.
failure_modes: Referring to 'the optimal solution' without uniqueness
rules.fix_template: Change “the” → “an” or add a uniqueness argument/citation.

rules.title: Avoid mixing informal commentary into the proof body
rules.check: Flag paragraphs inside proofs that indicate motivation/handwaving without labeling (e.g., “intuitively”, “we guess”, “it seems”).
rules.pass_criteria: Clearly separate rigorous argument from commentary via 'Remark', 'Idea of proof', or explicit labels.
exceptions: Proof sketch sections clearly labeled as sketches.
rules.fix_template: Move commentary into a Remark/Discussion or label it explicitly as a sketch; keep the proof body rigorous.
